evals.prompt: Create a 10-slide presentation about HashiCorp Terraform for IBM Cloud. Target audience is enterprise IT leaders evaluating infrastructure as code. Cover what Terraform is, key benefits, IBM Cloud provider capabilities, a customer success story with metrics, and next steps. Make it professional and on-brand.
evals.expected_output: A complete .pptx file with ~10 slides using IBM brand template, cover slide, section dividers, content slides with cards/visual enhancements, data slides with metrics and data visualizations, and IBM end slide
expectations: visual_density (≥2 visual elements per content slide)

evals.prompt: Build a short 6-slide deck introducing HashiCorp Vault secrets management to a security team. Include an overview of what Vault does, key features (dynamic secrets, encryption as a service, identity-based access), a comparison of Vault vs traditional secrets management, and a call to action. Use data callouts for key security statistics.
evals.expected_output: A complete .pptx file with ~6 slides using IBM brand template, appropriate use of data layouts/cards for statistics, proper HashiCorp/IBM color usage, data visualizations, and professional layout choices
expectations: visual_density (≥2 visual elements per content slide)

evals.prompt: I need a presentation about the full HashiCorp stack on IBM Cloud — Terraform, Vault, Consul, and Nomad working together. About 12 slides. Show how they integrate, include an architecture diagram slide placeholder, team contact slide with 3 people, and a comparison grid of the products. This is for a technical workshop audience.
evals.expected_output: A complete .pptx file with ~12 slides covering multiple HashiCorp products, using box/grid layouts for comparison, cards with shadows and accent bars, contacts layout for team, data visualizations, and varied content layouts throughout
expectations: visual_density (≥2 visual elements per content slide)
